messages.text: At Belmont the first suitor of rank was the Prince of Morocco. He asked Portia not to judge his appearance before his courage.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: The contract allows me no private choice. If you choose rightly, I am yours. If not, you must never marry.

messages.name: Morocco
messages.text: I accept. Gold suits what many desire, and Portia is a jewel the world desires.
He opened the golden casket and found a skull with a warning. Glittering surface had promised value and delivered emptiness.

messages.name: Morocco
messages.text: Cold lesson. I wanted what many men desire and did not deserve what one true heart must earn.
Later the Prince of Arragon chose silver because it promised what he deserved. He found a fool in the box and left ashamed.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: One chose by appearance, one by pride. If worth hides anywhere, perhaps it waits in lead.
While Belmont tested suitors, Venice hid another escape. Shylock kept his daughter Jessica in a house of locked doors and bitter rules.

messages.name: Jessica
messages.text: Lorenzo, I am ashamed to be my father's child only in manners. Tonight I will leave in disguise.

messages.name: Lorenzo
messages.text: Wear a page's clothes, bring what you can, and trust the night.
Jessica took jewels and money from Shylock, dressed as a boy, and climbed down to Lorenzo while music covered the risk.

messages.name: Jessica
messages.text: Love is blind, and I am glad. I would blush if the street could see this disguise.
The escape wounded Shylock more deeply because it mixed family, religion, money, and public shame.
Bassanio sailed toward Belmont with Gratiano beside him, unaware that the bond in Venice was tightening behind every mile.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Portia is not a prize to purchase. I must choose by worth, not by surface.

messages.name: Gratiano
messages.text: Then let lead look better than gold, or we are all undone.
In this story, bright things often misled. Gold failed, silver mocked, jewels vanished, and disguise carried both love and betrayal.
Only the lead casket remained closed, plain as duty and heavy as truth.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: If Bassanio comes, let music play while he chooses. My heart must obey a contract, even if it trembles.
Her servants prepared the hall, and every quiet object seemed to ask what love was worth when appearance had no answer.
Morocco left with ceremony, but disappointment followed him like a servant. Gold had taught him that public desire is a poor guide to private worth.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: A bright surface can invite the eye and still hide a warning. My father understood suitors too well.
Arragon was colder. He spoke of deserve as if love were a court salary, then found the portrait of a fool waiting for him.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: One man trusted appearance, the other trusted self-love. Lead begins to look almost friendly.
Jessica's escape moved through another kind of appearance. A daughter became a page, and a locked house became a stage for flight.

messages.name: Jessica
messages.text: I carry jewels, but what I want most is a different name spoken kindly in the street.
Portia watched each failed choice with relief and fear together. Relief because she remained free; fear because the right man might choose just as badly.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: The caskets teach, but they do not comfort. A lesson can still leave a woman waiting.
Portia could not stop the trials, but she could read the men who entered them. Their choices revealed what their speeches tried to hide.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: The caskets speak less than the suitors do before opening them.
By night, Belmont and Venice were both full of disguises, some chosen for love and some forced by law.
